comment_text.sn7.7:2.4: He betrays his prejudice in the last line.

root_text.sn7.7:1.2: Atha kho suddhikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn7.7:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn7.7:1.4: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho suddhikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.7:2.4: So sujjhati na aññā itarā pajā”ti.
root_text.sn7.7:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, suddhikabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.7:5.3: aññataro ca panāyasmā bhāradvājo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn7.7:1.2: Then the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Pure went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn7.7:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side,
translation_text.sn7.7:1.4: and spoke this verse in his presence:
translation_text.sn7.7:2.1: “No brahmin in the world is ever purified
translation_text.sn7.7:2.2: even though he’s ethical and mortifies himself.
translation_text.sn7.7:2.3: But one accomplished in knowledge and conduct
translation_text.sn7.7:2.4: is purified, not these other commoners.”
translation_text.sn7.7:3.3: if they’re filthy and corrupt within,
translation_text.sn7.7:4.1: Regardless of whether you’re an aristocrat,
translation_text.sn7.7:4.2: a brahmin, peasant, menial, <j>or a corpse-worker or scavenger—
translation_text.sn7.7:4.6: Know that for a fact, brahmin.”
translation_text.sn7.7:5.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Pure said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.7:5.3: And Venerable Bhāradvāja became one of the perfected.
